channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 6 people are injured after a truck crashed into a building and pedestrians in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A box truck crashed into a building Tuesday afternoon in Boston’s Chinatown neighborhood and hit several pedestrians before flipping onto its side, city officials said. The driver and five others were injured in what authorities said appears to have been an accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities amid heated nationwide debates over the participation of trans youth in athletics.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Anger against Trump is likely to cost the US international visitors, forecaster says
item.description: An influential travel forecasting company says anger over the Trump administration’s tariffs and rhetoric will likely cause international travel to the U.S. to fall even further than expected this year.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge rejects Trump administration’s bid to move Mahmoud Khalil’s legal case to Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that the legal battle over Mahmoud Khalil’s deportation should continue to play out in New Jersey, rejecting the Trump administration’s bid to transfer the Columbia University protester’s case to Louisiana. In a written decision Tuesday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Most immigrants at risk of deportation from US are Christian, report finds
item.description: As many as four in five immigrants at risk of deportation from the United States are Christian, according to a new report that calls on their fellow believers to consider the impact of the Trump administration's aggressive deportation policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court is at stake in a race that drew powerful political interests
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Majority control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court will be decided Tuesday in a race that broke records for spending and has become a proxy battle for the nation’s political fights, pitting a candidate backed by President Donald Trump against a Democratic-aligned challenger.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: Northern Michigan
item.description: The tip of Michigan's mitten struggled through another day without electricity Tuesday as restless residents tried to stay warm while utilities scrambled to restore power in a region waylaid by weekend freezing rain that brought down countless trees and poles.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Auto execs making last-ditch tariff appeal to the White House
item.description: The heads of auto companies Ford, GM and Stellantis are making a last-minute effort to appeal to the White House, NewsNation has learned.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Fire kills New York cat sanctuary founder and dozens of animals he rescued
item.description: A fire burned down a Long Island cat shelter, killing its founder, who lived there, and at least 59 of the felines he rescued, authorities said. The fire at the Happy Cat Sanctuary in the hamlet of Medford was reported shortly after 7 a.m. Monday. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi said Tuesday that she has directed prosecutors to seek the death penalty against Luigi Mangione in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, following through on the president's campaign promise to vigorously pursue capital punishment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's in Florida recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kidney donor detained by ICE before life-saving transplant can take place
item.description: Immigration rights activists are calling on ICE to release a Venezuelan migrant who was preparing to donate a kidney to his brother, who is in end-stage kidney failure, and has no criminal history.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, KRON4

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 60-ton crane stolen from south Columbus construction site
item.description: "Unfortunately, the keys had been left in it," Franklin County Sheriff's Office Sgt. Bill Duffer said.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, NewsNation

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Almost 40 percent of Americans report facing extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Nearly 40 percent of Americans experienced extreme weather last year — and those who did were more likely to be concerned about climate change, according to new polling.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Jersey state trooper accused of fatally shooting his dog, assaulting local police officer
item.description: A New Jersey state trooper allegedly shot and killed his dog, vandalized a stop sign and assaulted a local police officer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Evacuations ordered as winds pose challenges for firefighters battling California blaze
item.description: BISHOP, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters stopped the forward spread of a wildfire tearing through a remote area of eastern California, but evacuation orders for residents of hundreds of homes remained in place, officials said Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAA employee charged with assault after altercation in air traffic control tower at Reagan National Airport
item.description: An FAA employee was arrested and charged with assault after a scuffle in the air traffic control tower at Reagan Washington National Airport on March 27.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 20 staffers accused of abusing students or not reporting it at Pennsylvania charter school
item.description: Twenty staffers at a suburban Philadelphia charter school are facing charges related to the alleged physical abuse of students using painful, unapproved techniques to restrain and punish youths in a program meant to help them deal with emotional issues, authorities announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains found in California decades ago ID'd as missing New York 13-year-old
item.description: A 13-year-old girl who was found dead along a river in California's Santa Cruz County three decades ago was recently identified through DNA testing, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore blasted into orbit in June 2024, as part of a test flight of Boeing’s new Starliner crew capsule. They were scheduled to return about eight days later, but problems plagued the mission.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, ABC 4

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: 3 US soldiers found dead in armored vehicle in Lithuania; 1 still missing
item.description: Three of the U.S. Army soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have been found dead in their armored vehicle that was pulled from a swampy area early Monday, according to U.S. Army Europe and Africa Command. Another soldier is still missing.
item.dc:creator: By LIUDAS DAPKUS and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Apparent explosion, fire injures about a dozen in Detroit apartment building
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — About a dozen people have been injured, including six children, following an apparent explosion and fire Monday morning at a 2-story apartment building in Detroit. The explosion was reported about 4 a.m., according to fire officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Freezing rain brought down trees and power lines in Michigan and Wisconsin, cutting electricity for thousands of people Sunday in the upper Great Lakes region, while forecasters said severe weather was on its way to Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
